title: 4 Mark Dansk "Rytterkrone" - Frederik IV (Type 1)

obverse.description:
King on horse going to the right as in a parade. Frederik IV is wearing a long robe and a long scarf. He holds a scepter in is raised right arm.
Value in letters below, in exergue
obverse.lettering:
FRIDERICUS • IIII • D • G • REX • DAN • NOR • V • G •
______________________
✿ IIII ✿ MARCK •
DANSKE •
obverse.lettering_translation: Frederik IV Dei Gratia King of Denmark, Norway, the Wends and he Goths

reverse.description: Crowned bigger arms of Denmark within double Order Chain (of Danneborg and of the Order of the Elephant). Date below with mintmaster initials at sides. King's motto around rim.
reverse.lettering:
• DOMINUS MI  |  HI ADIUTOR •
✿ • C • 17 | 11 • W • ✿

comments:
DIFFERENCES BETWEEN 1711 RYTTERKRONE AND 1723:
Obverse:
- The 1723 horse is more detailed and realistic than that of 1711
- In coins of 1711 the legend is continuous while in 1723 coins the legend is cut by King's plumed hat.
- In coins of 1723 King wears a richly decorated cuirass


Reverse
- Differences in coat of arms (The inner shield change from four-parted to three-parted with the shift of Schleswig Arms) and crown shapes
- In 1723 coins the chains are circular and starts from the crown sides while in 1711 only the Elephant Order chain starts from crown ring
- In 1723 there is a man above elephant head in the pendant that is missing in 1711 coins
